tests.title: Correct build under test
tests.requires: You installed the release intended for this test pass.
tests.expected: The control panel version banner shows the build under test (0.10.3), so every result maps to the right code. A stale install must never masquerade as a bug.

steps.text: Open the control panel (http://127.0.0.1:8765) or the packaged app window.
Confirm the version banner reads 0.10.3 - it must match the installer you just ran.
If it does NOT match, STOP and install the correct build first - results from a stale build are invalid.

tests.title: Interactive crew: in-character crew voice segment, deterministic voice, barge-in, off-by-default (issue #69)
tests.requires: `[crew].enabled = true` (Settings -> Interactive crew, or 'turn crew on'). Distinct crew voices need the bus mixer (`[audio].enabled = true`) + a cast pool (default random ElevenLabs works); with no pool crew lines fall back to the persona voice. Optionally `[crew].roster = ["Nyx", "Vela"]`.
tests.expected: A crew-invoking prompt yields a reply where the crew member's line is in a distinct radio-filtered voice while the rest stays the companion's own clean voice; the same crew name keeps the same voice and different names differ; barge-in stops a crew line promptly across the persona->crew boundary; with crew off, replies are a single voice with no attribution and literal [bracketed] text is just read.

steps.text: Crew speaks in character: ask something that invites a crew voice ('have your sensor officer read off any contacts') -> the crew member's part is spoken in a distinct, radio-filtered voice, while the rest of the reply stays your companion's own clean voice. The persona is still the default speaker.
Deterministic voice: over several turns, the same crew name keeps the same voice, and different names sound different.
Barge-in mid-crew: while a crew line is speaking, tap cancel (`[`) -> it stops promptly and returns to Idle (barge-in works across the persona->crew segment boundary).
Off by default: with `[crew].enabled = false`, replies are spoken exactly as before - a single voice, no attribution, and any literal [bracketed] text is just read as text.

tests.title: Crew editor tab: add character, persistence, persona in conversation, assigned voice wins, stale-write guard (issue #70)
tests.requires: Run `run_covas_ui.py` and open the panel; click the crew tab. Edits `crew.json` (`[crew].file`), git-ignored. Personas fold into the system prompt; a chosen voice overrides the auto-assignment.
tests.expected: Adding a character writes crew.json and it persists across sessions; with crew on, that character responds in the personality you wrote in their assigned voice; a pinned voice is used every time (Auto reverts to the deterministic pick); a stale-write guard offers reload/overwrite (no silent clobber); with crew disabled the tab shows a banner but the roster still saves/loads.

steps.text: Add a character: click + ADD CHARACTER, enter a name (e.g. Nyx), a personality line, pick a Voice from the dropdown (or leave Auto), SAVE ROSTER -> a green Saved and a `crew.json` appears in the data dir with your entry.
Persists across sessions: restart the UI, reopen the Crew tab -> your roster is still there (loaded from `crew.json`).
Persona shows up in conversation: with crew on, ask something that invites that character -> they respond in the personality you wrote, in their assigned voice.
Assigned voice wins: pin a character to a specific voice, save, then invoke them a few times -> they use THAT voice every time. Switch back to Auto and save -> they revert to the deterministic voice.
Stale-write guard: open the Crew tab, hand-edit `crew.json` in a text editor and save, then click SAVE ROSTER -> it warns the file changed and offers reload or overwrite (no silent clobber).
Disabled banner: with `[crew].enabled = false`, the Crew tab shows a banner noting crew won't speak until enabled, but the roster still saves/loads.
